quality.rationale: 多步推导+宇宙学参数关联+辐射主导物理场景+完整解答

origin.question:
Problem 19.18. Solve the first-order Friedmann equation
$$
\left(\frac {\dot {\mathrm {R}}}{\bar {\mathrm {R}}} \right)^{2} = \frac {8 \pi \mathrm {G}}{3} \rho - \frac {\mathrm {k}}{\mathrm {R}^{2}}
$$
for $\mathbf{R}(t)$ when the density $ho$ is dominated by radiation. Express any parameters of the present epoch in terms of $\mathbf{H}_0$ and $\mathbf{q}_0$ .
origin.answer: $$R = \\sqrt{\\frac{8\\pi G \\rho_0 R_0^4}{3}} \\times \\begin{cases} \\sin \\eta & k=1 \\\\ \\eta & k=0 \\\\ \\sin h \\eta & k=-1 \\end{cases}, \\quad t = \\begin{cases} \\frac{1}{H_0} \\left[ \\frac{q_0^{1/2}}{q_0-1} \\right] (1-\\cos \\eta) & k=1 \\\\ \\frac{1}{2} H_0 R_0^2 \\eta^2 & k=0 \\\\ \\frac{1}{H_0} \\left[ \\frac{q_0^{1/2}}{1-q_0} \\right] (\\cos h \\eta - 1) & k=-1 \\end{cases}$$
origin.solution:
(b) When radiation dominates, the mass-energy in a given comoving volume is not constant. There is an extra decrease in density from the redshift of photons, so
$$
\rho = \rho_{0} \left(\frac {\mathbf {R}_{0}}{\mathbf {R}} \right)^{4}.
$$
The analog of Equation (2) is
$$
\left(\dot {\frac {\mathrm {R}}{\mathrm {R}}} \right)^{2} = \left(\frac {\mathrm {d R / d \eta}}{\mathrm {R}^{2}} \right)^{2} = \frac {8 \pi \mathrm {G}}{3} \rho_{0} \left(\frac {\mathrm {R}_{0}}{\mathrm {R}} \right)^{4} - \frac {\mathrm {k}}{\mathrm {R}^{2}}
$$
or
$$
\frac {\mathrm {d} \mathrm {R}}{\left(\frac {8}{3} \pi \mathrm {G} \rho_{0} \mathrm {R}_{0}^{4} - \mathrm {k R}^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}} = \mathrm {d} \eta
$$
which has solutions:
$$
R = \left(\frac {8 \pi}{3} G \rho_{0} R_{0}^{4}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \times \left\{\begin{array}{l l} \sin \eta & k = 1 \\ \eta & k = 0 \\ \sin h \eta & k = - 1. \end{array} \right. \tag {6}
$$
Instead of Equation (4), we now get (Problem 19.15)
$$
\mathbf {q}_{0} = \frac {8 \pi \mathrm {G}}{3} \frac {\rho_{0}}{\mathrm {H}_{0}^{2}},
$$
and instead of Equation (5), we have
$$
R_{0}^{2} = \frac {k}{\left(q_{0} - 1\right) H_{0}^{2}} \quad (k = \pm 1).
$$
Equation (6) therefore becomes
$$
\frac {8 \pi}{3} G \rho_{0} R_{0}^{4} = \left\{\begin{array}{c c} \frac {q_{0}}{(q_{0} - 1)^{2} H_{0}^{2}} & k = \pm 1 \\ H_{0}^{2} R_{0}^{4} & k = 0 \end{array} \right.
$$
and integrating for t gives
$$
t = \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} \frac {1}{H _ {0}} \left[ \frac {q _ {0} ^ {\frac {1}{2}}}{q _ {0} - 1} \right] & (1 - \cos \eta) \\ \frac {1}{2} H _ {0} R _ {0} ^ {2} \eta^ {2} & \\ \frac {1}{H _ {0}} \left[ \frac {q _ {0} ^ {\frac {1}{2}}}{1 - q _ {0}} \right] & (\cos h \eta - 1) \end{array} \quad k = 1 \right.
